Bulk Messenger

A personal/business sideload Android app for sending SMS to many people at once — the same message to everyone, or a different one per person — with automated, throttled delivery.

v1.0 Android 8.0+ (API 26) Kotlin + Jetpack Compose Sideload / not on Play Store Fully offline — no network permission
Overview Features Tech Stack Architecture Permissions Screenshots Testing Limitations
Overview

What it does, and why it exists

Bulk Messenger replaces the manual "open thread → type → send → repeat" workflow for anyone who regularly texts a list of numbers — event reminders, order updates, appointment nudges. Instead of a chat-by-chat slog, you write the message(s) once and a background worker sends them one at a time with a throttling delay, so the send pattern doesn't read as spam to the carrier or trip Android's own bulk-SMS warning.

It is intentionally built for sideload use, not Play Store distribution — that means it doesn't need to register as the device's default SMS handler, it keeps everything local (no backend, no analytics, no network permission at all), and every profile's data lives entirely on-device unless that profile explicitly exports a backup.

Features

Five feature areas, each with its own color

The four Home-screen actions each carry a distinct accent throughout the app — Job History's amber status chips, Personalized's rose row markers — so the feature you're in is always legible at a glance, not just from the screen title.

Broadcast

Same message → many numbers.

  • Pick contacts or type numbers manually
  • Live recipient list with per-entry removal
  • Send now, or schedule for a future date/time

Personalized Batch

A different message per number, as a two-page flow.

  • List page: every recipient as a card with a message preview
  • Dedicated add/edit page per recipient, with contact picker
  • CSV paste or real .csv file import, with a format example

Drafts

Reusable message templates.

  • Save, edit, delete
  • Title + body, reused across Broadcast sends

Job History

Every send is a job you can inspect and act on.

  • Per-number Sent / Failed / Pending status
  • Total sent + day-by-day breakdown
  • Message snippet on the card; per-item snippet when personalized
  • Filter by All / Broadcast / Personalized
  • Retry Failed — reappears only when a job has failures, resends just those, with a SIM picker on dual-SIM devices

Multi-user profiles

First-launch onboarding creates a profile (name + color). Switch profiles from Home; each profile has its own drafts, job history, theme, and default SIM — nothing leaks across profiles.

Dual-SIM support

A SIM picker appears on Broadcast, Personalized, and Settings only when 2+ active SIMs are detected — invisible otherwise. The chosen SIM is remembered as the profile's default.

Reliability

Sending promotes the worker to a foreground service with a live progress notification, so aggressive battery managers (Vivo/Oppo/Realme) are far less likely to kill a send mid-flight. A one-tap battery-exemption shortcut lives in Settings.

Backup & restore

Everything is local-only, so Settings has an explicit JSON export/import via the system file picker — including a restore option right on the first-launch screen for a fresh install.

Tech Stack

What it's built with, and why

Language
Kotlin— coroutines make the throttled, cancellable send loop straightforward
UI
Jetpack Compose Material 3— per-profile theming (System/Light/Dark) without hand-rolled theme plumbing Navigation Compose— nested graph shares one ViewModel between the Personalized list and its row editor
Architecture
MVVM— Screens → ViewModel → Repository, keeping Room/WorkManager out of the UI layer AndroidViewModel + StateFlow
Persistence
Room— UserProfile, Draft, BulkJob, BulkJobItem, all queries scoped by userId SharedPreferences— just the active-profile pointer, readable before Room's first query resolves
Background work
WorkManager— survives process death and device reboot; delayed enqueue powers scheduled sends Foreground Service— keeps the sender alive against aggressive OEM battery managers
System APIs
SmsManager SubscriptionManager— dual-SIM detection Storage Access Framework— CSV import, backup export/import, no raw filesystem paths core-splashscreen
Testing
JUnit4 + AndroidX Test— instrumented, run on-device Compose UI Test Room in-memory DB— backup/restore and retry logic tested against a real (ephemeral) database
Architecture

Data flow, screen to database

A send request moves from Compose UI down through a ViewModel and repository, splits into an immediate Room write and a WorkManager enqueue, then the worker drives SmsManager directly — no server in the loop anywhere.

Compose ScreenBroadcast / Personalized
→
ViewModelBroadcast/Personalized VM
→
RepositoryMessageRepository
→
RoomBulkJob + BulkJobItem rows
WorkManagerenqueue SmsSendWorker
→
SmsSendWorkerforeground service, throttled loop
→
SmsManagerper-item send, per-SIM if chosen

Every screen additionally reads through SessionViewModel, which owns the active profile and scopes every Room query by userId.

Permissions

Every permission, and what breaks without it

No permission here does anything beyond what's listed — there's no analytics SDK, no ad framework, and no network permission of any kind in the manifest.

PermissionUsed forIf denied
SEND_SMS Actually sending messages via SmsManager. App shows a blocking "grant permission" screen — this one is required to use the app at all.
READ_CONTACTS The "Pick from Contacts" picker on Broadcast and Personalized. Picker is skipped; you can still type numbers manually.
READ_PHONE_STATE Detecting active SIMs via SubscriptionManager for the dual-SIM picker. SIM picker never appears; sends use the device default SIM.
POST_NOTIFICATIONS The sending-progress and job-complete notifications (Android 13+). Sends still run; you just don't see the progress/completion notification.
FOREGROUND_SERVICE
FOREGROUND_SERVICE_DATA_SYNC		 Promoting the sender to a foreground service while actively sending. Not user-revocable (install-time permission) — required for the reliability fix to function.
REQUEST_IGNORE_BATTERY_OPTIMIZATIONS The "Disable battery optimization" shortcut in Settings. Not user-revocable — only gates whether the shortcut can open the system dialog.

Testing

Coverage

Full test-case matrix lives in docs/TEST_CASES.md — the summary below is what actually ran this pass.

50+
manual QA cases documented
3 / 3
instrumented tests passed on-device
0
real SMS sent by automated tests
Instrumented testWhat it proves
BackupRestoreInstrumentedTest Export → wipe → import round-trips every table (profiles, drafts, jobs, items) byte-for-byte against an in-memory Room DB.
RetryFailedItemsInstrumentedTest Retry resets only FAILED items to PENDING (clearing the error), leaves SENT items untouched, and requeues the job.
HomeScreenTest Compose UI test — all four action cards render and are clickable.
Known Limitations

What this build doesn't do yet

Carrier

Daily SMS caps aren't ours to control. Carriers commonly cap outbound SMS per SIM per day; the app automates the clicking, not the carrier's own throttling.

Timing

Scheduled sends aren't exact-alarm precise. WorkManager's delayed enqueue can fire a few minutes late under Doze or aggressive OEM battery management — the battery-exemption shortcut in Settings mitigates this.

Delivery

No delivery-report tracking yet. Status reflects send success/failure, not whether the recipient's device confirmed receipt.

Backup

Auto Backup is deliberately opted out. The Room database is excluded from Android's implicit cloud backup; recovery relies on the explicit Backup/Restore feature instead.
